source.description: One reviewed six-panel raster contact sheet cropped into five process masters and a final typewriter master.
source.notes: Keep the same front three-quarter compact typewriter, sheet of paper, key rows, roller, and carriage-return lever in every panel.

process_strategy: The one-sheet pencil progression builds a boxy typewriter from its baseline and body silhouette through paper, keys, and hardware; the final only strengthens existing lines and quiet tones.

final_elements.name: typewriter body
final_elements.description: A compact rounded-front rectangular machine body on a low base.

final_elements.name: paper sheet and roller
final_elements.description: A slightly tilted paper rectangle rising from a narrow roller bar.

final_elements.name: key bank
final_elements.description: Three staggered rows of small round key marks across the body front.

final_elements.name: space bar and front lip
final_elements.description: A long center bar and a narrow lower face edge beneath the keys.

final_elements.name: carriage lever and side knob
final_elements.description: A short bent lever on the right and circular side knob on the roller.

final_elements.name: soft cast shadow and warm-gray tone
final_elements.description: A soft shadow under the base and restrained warm-gray pencil on existing body planes.

frames.visible_job: Draw a low baseline and the compact boxy body silhouette.
Add the narrow roller bar and one simple paper sheet behind the established body.
Place three staggered rows of loose round key marks on the existing front plane.
Add a long space bar and lower front lip beneath the established key rows.
Add the short carriage lever, side knob, soft shadow, and warm-gray tone to existing forms.
introduces: soft cast shadow and warm-gray tone

final_step.visible_job: Only darken established contours, keys, paper edge, roller, lever, knob, and existing quiet tone and shadow.

rejection_checks: Reject if the paper, key rows, lever, knob, or body proportions drift between panels.
Reject if a major typewriter control, paper sheet, or shadow first appears in the final panel.
Reject a polished illustration or a vector-like machine; the finish must remain handmade pencil on paper.
